resources.rationale: Oracle documents Always Free A1 as 2 OCPUs and 12 GiB total. The required 4/24 profile exceeds that limit; read-only plan review is allowed after acknowledgement, but apply requires a new paid/non-billable entitlement approval and source revision.

Temporary x86_64 bridge while A1 capacity is unavailable. The first release reuses the existing private-addressed primary and stopped recovery fallback because the tenancy rejects additional Core Instance creation. No instance is deleted or replaced. Apply requires an explicit approval reference, a future expiry no more than 90 days away, and a reviewed source/plan bound to the E5 profile.

Steady state remains one RUNNING and one STOPPED E5 4/24 instance. Recovery drills start the stopped fallback only for a bounded exercise and restore STOPPED in finally. The rollout creates no additional compute and terminates none.
resources.resource: retained primary and stopped recovery compute

resources.rationale: Two retained 200 GiB boot volumes plus one 130 GiB data volume total 530 GiB. Stopped instances continue to retain boot-volume storage.
resources.resource: retained boot and data volumes totaling 530 GiB

resources.rationale: Oracle documents 200 GB combined boot and block volume Always Free storage in the home region; existing tenancy usage must be inspected before plan approval.

resources.resource: DEFAULT Vault with software-protected AES key

resources.rationale: OCI VCN control-plane resources and Network Load Balancer have no direct hourly resource charge; public IPv4, traffic and external services remain separately reviewed.
resources.resource: VCN, NSG, NAT/Service/Internet Gateways, public Network Load Balancer, IAM
OCI Bastion and Run Command management path
